item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Experimentation utilizing Class D personnel fitted with GPS tracking devices has not been successful.

can this be elaborated on?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice. The karma of the two crooks ending up as toys for (presumably giant) alien/extra-dimensional kids was priceless. The mother's prim tone in complaining about what amounts to disobedient slaves also makes for great dark humor.

Only real gripe is that there is room for more build-up to that twist. (Giant hairbrushes and pens getting warped in from "the other side", as others mentioned, could work; maybe giant fingerprints/claw marks on the walls, from the brats grabbing their new toys?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Why would a mother leave a complaint note inside the dollhouse?" is a good question… and now I think I've thought of a good answer.

It's a convenient way of attaching the note when you ship the product back to the manufacturer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, yes it is

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved this. The end wasn't spoiled for me by a title, and the ending was really great. I had been enjoying the skip, but the punchline was fantastic. It took me a few moments, but it was quite enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it… But… I dont like the twist ending.

Some nit picks and suggestions
Hope these help

1. This reeks of deus ex machina to me; Why would a mother leave a complaint note inside the dollhouse?

2. If it hadnt been for the mainlist title hints I wouldnt have got it. I think the article needs to be able to stand up on its own. Maybe having abducted people returned with new clothing that are scaled up Barbie jackets or whatever. Maybe an accessory that only comes with certain dolls, you get the jist. Just one clue in the article hinting towards dolls would be enough. The taped on head was a little too subtle, but works well in the article.

3. Maybe other things could turn up in the doll house or things could be slightly arranged. A drawing pin 100 times larger than normal, a hair thats several millimeters thick or a blood sample with massive blood cells in it. Furniture could be rearranged for common dolls house events such as parties or meals, etc.

Not sure exactly how to improve but it needs it before I upvote I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, I'm the opposite, the mainlist title spoiled the ending for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like that old Twilight Zone episode… And I like it. Upvoted hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I came to this conclusion as well. I like that it simply led me directly to this twist, without any other details to ponder over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what I got from this is that the house is actually a toy dollhouse for some extra dimensional alien-type creatures, and whenever two people enter the house, they are transformed into dolls and taken to the other dimension. Is this correct?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. You should make the twist a little more obvious because a lot of people in the comments including myself didn't get it. A picture wouldn't hurt either. Also you should specify that the doors shouldn't be closed and that personnel shouldn't be co-ed in the beginning. Other than that I really liked it. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooooo, I'm really liking that last note. I was waiting for some kind of twist, didn't expect that. Good work, upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The twist sold it for me. Up to that point I had been furiously meh-ing at a house that makes people inside disappear.
Though I do agree that now TheLoyalSubject mentioned it, I can't get the Doctor Who reference out of my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, so, it's an extradimensional dollhouse?

Like this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take a look at the article's title in the list, it may help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OK, I don't get it. Explain like I'm five someone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple but cute. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The twist ending seems to have flown right over my head, because I can't for the life of me how it makes the rest of the article more interesting.

E: And despite eluding me for a good five minutes, I only worked it out 20 seconds after posting this. I'll cancel my downvote, because my strong objections to this were founded in stupidity. I'll come back and re-vote on this later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, that twist. That being said, I'm feeling neutral about this one, because the ending is the only interesting aspect. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's short, and it's simple, very simple,and I like it. I also agree with the other comments. It needs a punch, and what it has now is a tap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs more of a kick to it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's got a lot of fairly standard hallmarks, but I disagree with UraniumEmpire: the ending does clinch it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The effects aren't particularly interesting, and the twist ending doesn't make up for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has the aura of being clichèish, but I don't think it's actually that clichè. That being said, I found the article as a whole to be a bit bland and predictable. You made it just interesting enough and well written that I don't feel like I can downvote in good consious, but I also don't like it enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the last part. It's unusual but ties it all together.
